cards.back: Q = 2π × (energy stored in circuit) / (energy dissipated per cycle). Universal for any resonant system.
wL(t) = (Vm²L/2R²)cos²(ωot). Maximum at t=0, zero at t=T/4.
wC(t) = (Vm²L/2R²)sin²(ωot). Maximum at t=T/4, zero at t=0.
wS = wL + wC = Vm²L/(2R²) = CONSTANT. cos² + sin² = 1.
ωoL/R, 1/ωoCR, ωo/B, 2πwS/wD, VL/Vs (series), IL/Is (parallel). All equal.
VC = Q × Vs. With Q=200, Vs=10V → VC=2000V. Components must be rated for Q×Vs, not just Vs.

cards.front: Why does real inductor reduce Q?
cards.back: Wire resistance adds to total R. Q = ωoL/R_total < ωoL/R_external. Parasitic R always degrades Q.
Like pendulum: inductor = kinetic energy (current), capacitor = potential energy (voltage). Total constant at resonance.
Q=5-10: broad/unselective. Q=50-100: moderate. Q=200-500: radio receivers. Q>10000: crystal oscillators.
Decrease R (less loss), increase L (more stored energy), or use higher-quality components. ω₀ doesn't change.
